channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: A Look at Katharine Ross's Career and Style in the 1960s
item.description: Katharine Ross, born on January 29, 1940, became one of the defining faces of American cinema in the 1960s. Known for her natural beauty and understated acting style, she transitioned from television ...

item.title: Tanzania Strengthens Teachers’ Digital Skills to Improve Education
item.description: Tanzania government is making big moves to equip teachers with digital skills as part of efforts to improve education for all students across the country. Recently, in Dodoma, a four-day ICT Competenc...

item.title: A Retrospective Look at the TV Sitcom 'Bewitched' and Its Cultural Impact
item.description: “Bewitched,” created by Sol Saks and produced by Screen Gems, remains one of the most iconic American television shows, airing on ABC from 1964 to 1972. Starring Elizabeth Montgomery as Samantha Steph...

item.title: Record 274 climbers reach Everest summit from Nepal side in a single day
item.description: On Wednesday, a record 274 people successfully reached the summit of Mount Everest from the Nepal side, marking the busiest single day ever on the mountain's southern route. The unusually crowded summ...

item.title: Albanese willing to adjust trust tax rules but maintains capital gains tax reforms
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has indicated a willingness to adjust certain aspects of Labor's 2026 federal budget, particularly regarding the taxation of discretionary testamentary trusts. These tr...

item.title: Muhammad Ali and Etta James Meet During Zaire 74 Festival
item.description: In September 1974, boxing legend Muhammad Ali met acclaimed singer Etta James in Kinshasa, Zaire (now the Democratic Republic of the Congo), just before the iconic “Rumble in the Jungle” fight against...

item.title: Authorities Urged to Intervene After Clashes Between Locals and Foreign Nationals at Diakonia Centre, Durban
item.description: Tensions flared outside Durban's Diakonia Centre when over 100 mostly Congolese nationals sought refuge there amid threats linked to rising protests against undocumented migrants in the city. What beg...

item.title: Trump Administration Plans $2 Billion in Grants to Quantum Computing Companies with Federal Equity Stakes
item.description: The Trump administration is set to announce a significant $2 billion investment in quantum computing through the U.S. Department of Commerce. The funding will be distributed to nine companies working ...

item.title: TUI withdraws sponsorship from Married At First Sight programmes following serious sexual assault allegations
item.description: Holiday company TUI has decided to end its sponsorship of the reality television show Married At First Sight in its UK, Australian, and US versions. This decision follows a BBC Panorama investigation ...

item.title: U.S. Questions NATO Role Following Lack of Support During Iran Conflict
item.description: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio stated that the Trump administration is reassessing its commitment to NATO after some allies did not provide support during the Iran war. Rubio, en route to a NATO ...

item.title: Cristiano Ronaldo helps Al Nassr secure first Saudi Pro League championship since 2019
item.description: Cristiano Ronaldo don finally help Al Nassr lift their first Saudi Pro League trophy since 2019, ending a seven-year dry spell for domestic glory. The Portuguese superstar shine for the Riyadh club as...

item.title: Reflections on Post-Colonial Ghana and the Lessons for Modern Africa
item.description: Orapeleng Matshediso reviews Ayi Kwei Armah’s seminal novel, 'The Beautyful Ones Are Not Yet Born', written ten years after Ghana gained independence. The novel exposes the social decay, corruption, a...

item.title: Gaza flotilla activists arrive in Turkey, report mistreatment by Israeli forces
item.description: A group of activists involved in the Gaza flotilla arrived safely in Istanbul, Turkey, after a tense confrontation at sea with Israeli forces. Wearing grey tracksuits and Arab keffiyehs, the activists...

item.title: FG launches GovGuideNigeria AI platform to simplify access to government services
item.description: The Federal Government of Nigeria don roll out new AI-powered platform wey dem call GovGuideNigeria. The platform go make am easier for Nigerians to get government information and public services anyt...

item.title: The History and Legacy of Girlschool, Pioneering All-Female British Rock Band
item.description: Girlschool is a British rock band that emerged during the new wave of British heavy metal in 1978. Initially formed from a school band called Painted Lady, the band gained significant attention in the...

item.title: US Confident of Concluding India Trade Deal Soon, Says Ambassador
item.description: The United States has expressed optimism about concluding a bilateral trade agreement with India, according to US Ambassador Sergio Gor. Speaking at an event organised by the American Chamber of Comme...

item.title: David Pope shares his editorial cartoons on ageing at home
item.description: This piece presents the editorial perspective of David Pope, the Canberra Times' cartoonist, focusing on the theme of 'ageing in place.' Pope provides a satirical lens on contemporary news, using humo...

item.title: Study finds thawing permafrost contaminating northern Canadian rivers
item.description: A recent study has revealed that northern rivers in Canada are increasingly affected by the thawing of permafrost, which exposes ancient bedrock and releases toxic metals into the water. Researchers r...

item.title: Ghana Immigration Service Trains Officers Ahead of Nationwide E-Visa Rollout
item.description: The Ghana Immigration Service (GIS) is preparing for the nationwide launch of Ghana's electronic visa (e-visa) system, scheduled for May 25, 2026. To ensure smooth implementation, GIS recently conduct...

item.title: James Carville Advocates Targeting Republican Party Switchers in the Southern U.S.
item.description: Democratic strategist James Carville has proposed a new initiative aimed at attracting Republicans in the traditionally conservative Southern states to the Democratic Party. Speaking on his Politicon ...
